text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY -- ON-STUDY FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Phase II Clinical Trial: Savolitinib + Tagrisso for MET-Amplified EGFR-Mutant NSCLC Post-Third-Generation TKI Progression

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Margaret Yuen is a 64-year-old East Asian (Cantonese) woman with Stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, currently on protocol therapy with savolitinib 300 mg daily plus Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily, presenting for her 8-week on-trial restaging visit. She comes in today reporting improved energy and substantially reduced lower extremity swelling since the addition of furosemide. She is generally tolerating the combination well and is encouraged by her recent imaging results.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Yuen is a 64-year-old former smoker (12 pack-year history, quit approximately 20 years ago) originally diagnosed with Stage IIIB NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, harboring an EGFR exon 21 L858R mutation (allele frequency 38.2% on initial FoundationOne CDx). She was initially managed with Tagrisso monotherapy, which produced a robust and durable partial response lasting approximately 14 months. At that point she developed systemic progression -- growth of the right upper lobe primary and a new right paratracheal node -- as well as three new brain metastases (left frontal 12 mm, right cerebellar 8 mm, left parietal 6 mm) associated with new-onset bifrontal headaches and transient word-finding difficulties.

At progression, liquid biopsy (Guardant360) identified acquired EGFR T790M at low allele frequency (0.6%), and tissue re-biopsy with repeat FoundationOne CDx revealed a more complex polyclonal resistance landscape: low-level T790M (tissue AF 3.1%), high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.1, FISH-confirmed), and newly acquired PIK3CA p.E545K and PTEN p.R130Q alterations. She underwent Gamma Knife SRS to all three brain lesions without complication, followed by a bridging course of carbo (AUC 5) plus peme (Alimta) for 4 cycles with good tolerability and partial response in the chest (primary tumor decreased from 2.7 cm to 2.1 cm). With high-level MET amplification identified as the likely dominant resistance driver, the molecular tumor board recommended enrollment on this Phase II trial combining savolitinib (a MET inhibitor) with osi rechallenge.

She was started on protocol therapy approximately 8 weeks ago. At week 4 she developed grade 2 bilateral lower extremity pitting edema to the mid-shins, a recognized savolitinib-related toxicity, without evidence of cardiac or hepatic etiology (echocardiogram at that time showed preserved LV function, EF 60-65%, no pericardial effusion). Furosemide 20 mg orally daily was initiated with a good response; edema improved to grade 1 within 3 weeks. She also had return of her acneiform rash at grade 1 with osi rechallenge, which is manageable with topical therapy.

Today she presents for 8-week protocol restaging. Imaging results reviewed (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast and MRI brain with and without gadolinium). Key findings:
- Right upper lobe mass decreased from 2.1 cm (at trial entry) to 1.4 cm -- a 33% reduction from trial baseline, and representing a 76% reduction from original diagnostic measurement of 5.8 cm.
- Right paratracheal lymph node is no longer measurable on CT.
- MRI brain: Left frontal lesion further decreased to 4 mm with resolution of surrounding vasogenic edema; right cerebellar lesion stable at 4 mm; left parietal lesion is no longer visualized.
- No new intracranial lesions. No leptomeningeal enhancement.
- No new disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

Overall assessment: Confirmed partial response on protocol, with excellent CNS response as well. The near-complete resolution of the left parietal lesion and dramatic reduction in the left frontal lesion are particularly encouraging and likely reflect both the ongoing effect of prior SRS and the CNS penetration of Tagrisso.

Protocol-mandated ctDNA correlative sample and peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) samples collected today per study requirements.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Fatigue grade 1, improved compared to during chemotherapy. Appetite is modestly reduced but stable. No unintentional weight loss in the past 4 weeks; weight has been stable. No fevers or chills.
Neurological: Headaches have resolved. Word-finding difficulties have fully resolved since SRS and steroid taper. No new neurological symptoms. No seizures. No vision changes. No focal weakness or sensory changes.
Respiratory: Cough is minimal and non-productive. No hemoptysis. No significant dyspnea at rest; mild exertional dyspnea with vigorous activity (e.g., climbing two flights of stairs), unchanged from recent baseline.
Cardiovascular: Lower extremity edema significantly improved on furosemide -- she rates it much better than 4 weeks ago. No orthopnea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. No palpitations.
Gastrointestinal: Mild nausea grade 1, intermittent, managed with dietary modification. No vomiting. Stools are regular, one formed bowel movement daily, no diarrhea currently. Appetite mildly reduced.
Musculoskeletal: No bone pain. No new musculoskeletal complaints.
Skin: Grade 1 papulopustular facial rash consistent with EGFR TKI class effect, similar to original osi therapy. Mild paronychia right index finger, grade 1. Using topical clindamycin lotion.
Endocrine/metabolic: No symptoms of hypo- or hyperglycemia. No steroid-related symptoms (patient completed dexamethasone taper 6+ weeks ago).
Psychosocial: She reports acceptable quality of life overall. She is currently teaching part-time and values maintaining this activity. She is cautiously optimistic given the imaging response.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY (previously documented; reviewed and updated)
1. Stage IV NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, EGFR L858R-mutant -- as detailed above
2. Prior brain metastases (left frontal, right cerebellar, left parietal) -- s/p Gamma Knife SRS, now responding
3. Chronic small vessel ischemic disease -- incidental finding on brain MRI, clinically silent
4. Former smoker (12 pack-year history, quit ~20 years ago)
5. No prior history of interstitial lung disease, autoimmune disease, or inflammatory bowel disease

SOCIAL HISTORY
Born in Hong Kong, immigrated to the United States at age 32. Lives with her husband. Two adult children. High school mathematics teacher, currently working part-time. Non-drinker. Non-smoker for approximately 20 years. Denies recreational drug use. Adequate social support.

FAMILY HISTORY
Paternal uncle: Lung cancer (histology unknown, deceased). No other known first-degree relatives with malignancy. No known hereditary cancer syndromes.

ALLERGIES
No known drug allergies.

MEDICATIONS (current)
1. Savolitinib 300 mg orally once daily (on trial)
2. Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally once daily (on trial)
3. Furosemide 20 mg orally once daily -- for savolitinib-related peripheral edema
4. Folic acid 400 mcg orally once daily -- Alimta supplementation (continuing per guideline; last peme dose approximately 6-7 weeks ago)
5. Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM every 9 weeks -- Alimta supplementation (per standard protocol; last dose given prior to cycle 3 of chemotherapy)
6. Topical clindamycin 1% solution to face -- for EGFR TKI rash
7. Urea-based moisturizer -- skin care with EGFR TKI therapy
8. Ondansetron (Zofran) 8 mg orally as needed -- for nausea (using approximately 2x/week)
9. Acetaminophen 650 mg orally as needed -- for residual mild headache (using rarely, 1-2x/week)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Ms. Yuen is a well-appearing, pleasant 64-year-old East Asian woman in no acute distress. Alert and fully oriented. Communicating clearly, no anomic aphasia noted today (tested with naming task; appropriate). Appears well-nourished.
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 118/72 mmHg, heart rate 74 bpm and regular, respiratory rate 16/min, temperature 98.2°F (36.8°C), oxygen saturation 96% on room air. Weight 56.1 kg (stable compared to last visit 4 weeks ago; down approximately 1 kg from trial enrollment baseline). Height 158 cm. BMI 22.5 kg/m2.
Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis. Pupils equal and reactive to light bilaterally. No scleral icterus.
Skin: Grade 1 papulopustular acneiform rash on face (forehead and malar regions), mild. Minimal erythema, no vesicles or pustules requiring intervention. Grade 1 paronychia right index finger with mild periungual erythema, no purulent discharge. No other skin findings.
Neck: No palpable cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Previously palpable right supraclavicular node is no longer appreciated -- this has been absent since her initial response to osi and has remained absent throughout.
Chest/Lungs: Chest expansion symmetric. Percussion resonant bilaterally. Auscultation reveals mildly decreased breath sounds at the right apex, unchanged from prior examinations, consistent with known disease site and prior treatment changes. No crackles, wheezes, or pleural rub.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly appreciated. Bowel sounds present and active in all quadrants.
Extremities: Bilateral lower extremity pitting edema, 1+ to the ankles bilaterally (grade 1, improved from 2+ to mid-shins 4 weeks ago). No calf tenderness. No erythema or warmth suggestive of DVT. Good capillary refill.
Neurological: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 in all extremities. No pronator drift. Sensation grossly intact. Coordination: Finger-to-nose and heel-to-shin intact bilaterally. Gait steady, normal stride. No evidence of cerebellar dysfunction.
Lymph nodes: No axillary, inguinal, or other peripheral lymphadenopathy palpated beyond the above notation re: neck.

---

RELEVANT LABORATORY RESULTS
Complete Blood Count (drawn today):
- WBC: 5.8 x 10^9/L (normal)
- ANC: 3.6 x 10^9/L (normal; no neutropenia)
- Hemoglobin: 11.2 g/dL (mild anemia, grade 1, stable, attributed to prior chemotherapy and chronic disease)
- Platelets: 198 x 10^9/L (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (drawn today):
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L, Potassium: 3.7 mEq/L (within normal limits; monitoring given furosemide use)
- Creatinine: 0.72 mg/dL, eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2 (stable renal function)
- AST: 28 U/L, ALT: 31 U/L (normal; no hepatotoxicity)
- Total bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL (normal)
- Alkaline phosphatase: 74 U/L (normal)
- Albumin: 3.8 g/dL (adequate nutritional status)
- LDH: 172 U/L (within normal limits)

Additional labs:
- Magnesium: 1.9 mg/dL (low-normal; will monitor given furosemide)
- INR: 1.0
- TSH: 1.8 mIU/L (normal; checked as baseline given TKI therapy)

Note: Corrected QTc on recent ECG (obtained at week 4 protocol visit): 432 ms (within acceptable range; savolitinib can have QT prolongation potential; ongoing monitoring per protocol).

---

IMAGING RESULTS
CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast (obtained this week, reviewed in clinic today):
Right upper lobe: The previously noted residual right upper lobe mass has continued to shrink and now measures approximately 1.4 cm in longest dimension, down from 2.1 cm at trial entry eight weeks ago. This represents roughly a one-third reduction since starting the trial combination, and puts the overall reduction from her original 5.8 cm diagnostic mass at approximately 76%. The lesion appears as a focal irregular opacity with surrounding parenchymal distortion, likely reflecting a mix of residual tumor and post-treatment fibrosis -- a common finding at this stage. No chest wall invasion is identified currently.
Mediastinum/lymph nodes: The right paratracheal lymph node that had been enlarged at 1.1 cm at progression (and was 0.7 cm at the end of chemotherapy) is no longer measurable on today's scan -- essentially resolved. Right hilar nodes remain small, within normal limits. No new mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.
Pleural spaces: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax.
Abdomen and pelvis: Liver is normal in size with no focal hepatic lesions. Adrenal glands are normal bilaterally -- no new adrenal lesions. Kidneys unremarkable. No pelvic masses or lymphadenopathy. No ascites.
Overall chest impression: Continued favorable response of the right upper lobe primary and complete normalization of mediastinal nodal disease. No new sites of thoracic, abdominal, or pelvic disease.

MRI Brain with and without Gadolinium (obtained this week, reviewed in clinic today):
Left frontal lobe: The treated metastasis (originally 12 mm at time of SRS) has further decreased to approximately 4 mm, with complete resolution of the surrounding vasogenic edema that was present at initial diagnosis. The lesion enhances faintly -- likely a mix of treatment effect and residual small lesion, consistent with post-SRS change and/or ongoing systemic response from osi CNS penetration.
Right cerebellar hemisphere: Previously measured at 8 mm at time of SRS, 6 mm at 3-cycle chemotherapy restaging. Stable today at approximately 4 mm, with a small area of faint enhancement. No surrounding edema.
Left parietal lobe: The 6-mm lesion originally treated with SRS is no longer visualized on today's MRI. This is consistent with complete local treatment effect (SRS) and/or systemic response.
No new enhancing intracranial lesions identified. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No hydrocephalus. No midline shift. Ventricles and sulci appear normal for age. The scattered periventricular T2/FLAIR hyperintensities noted on prior imaging (consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic disease) are unchanged.
Brain impression: Favorable CNS response -- one lesion has resolved, one is stable, and the dominant left frontal lesion has reduced dramatically. No evidence of new CNS metastatic disease or leptomeningeal involvement.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, EGFR L858R-mutant with MET amplification (acquired resistance to Tagrisso), brain metastases s/p SRS -- currently on Phase II trial of savolitinib 300 mg daily plus osi 80 mg daily.

Ms. Yuen is doing very well clinically and radiographically at her 8-week on-trial assessment. Today's imaging confirms a clear and ongoing partial response in both the chest and the brain. The right upper lobe mass has shrunk to 1.4 cm, representing a 76% reduction from her original diagnostic size. Mediastinal nodes have normalized. On brain MRI, two of three previously treated metastases have continued to respond (one has resolved completely), and no new intracranial lesions are present. These findings are highly encouraging and support continued efficacy of the MET inhibitor/osi rechallenge strategy in this patient, whose dominant resistance mechanism at progression was MET amplification.

The response pattern is consistent with what has been described in early reports of savolitinib plus Tagrisso for MET-amplified, EGFR-mutant NSCLC post-TKI progression -- a biologically rational combination addressing both the bypass pathway and the underlying EGFR oncogene. The excellent CNS response is at least partly attributable to osi's well-established blood-brain barrier penetration.

Plan:
- Continue savolitinib 300 mg daily plus osi 80 mg daily per protocol. No dose modifications required. Full doses maintained.
- Continue furosemide 20 mg daily for grade 1 peripheral edema (well-controlled). Will check potassium and magnesium at next visit to ensure electrolytes remain stable on furosemide; today's potassium is 3.7 mEq/L (acceptable). If hypokalemia develops, will supplement.
- Grade 1 acneiform rash and paronychia: Continue topical clindamycin 1% solution to face. Urea-based moisturizer continued. Paronychia: warm water soaks and topical antibiotic as needed. No dose interruption required for grade 1 skin toxicity.
- Grade 1 nausea: Continue ondansetron as needed. No dietary changes beyond what she is already doing.
- Protocol-mandated ctDNA and PBMC correlative samples collected today per protocol. Sent to sponsor's designated laboratory.
- ECG to be repeated at next visit per protocol QTc monitoring schedule; today's QTc 432 ms is acceptable.
- Continue vitamin B12 supplementation per Alimta protocol schedule (even though peme is now discontinued, standard practice to continue the cycle-based supplementation through the scheduled doses). Folic acid ongoing.
- Next on-trial restaging CT/MRI scheduled in 8 weeks (16 weeks from trial enrollment).
- Patient will call clinic or the trial research nurse coordinator if she develops new neurological symptoms, dyspnea, worsening edema, skin rash grade ≥2, or any other concerning new symptoms between visits.

2. Brain metastases s/p Gamma Knife SRS -- responding.
All three lesions are responding, with one now undetectable on MRI. No new intracranial disease. No leptomeningeal involvement. Neurological exam today is completely normal with resolution of prior word-finding difficulties. No additional radiation or neurosurgical intervention indicated at this time. If the residually enhancing left frontal and right cerebellar lesions show growth on future imaging, will need to distinguish radiation necrosis from tumor progression (perfusion MRI or MR spectroscopy may be considered at that point). Neurosurgical oncology follow-up is deferred for now.

3. Anemia (hemoglobin 11.2 g/dL, grade 1).
Mild anemia, likely multifactorial (prior chemotherapy, chronic disease). Stable compared to recent prior values. No symptoms of anemia (no dyspnea at rest, no lightheadedness). No intervention indicated currently. Will recheck CBC at next visit.

4. Peripheral edema (grade 1, improved).
Well-controlled on furosemide 20 mg daily. Cardiac etiology excluded by echocardiogram. Consistent with savolitinib class effect. Will monitor electrolytes.

5. Performance status and quality of life.
ECOG PS 1. She reports acceptable quality of life and has returned to teaching mathematics part-time, which is meaningful to her. She is cautiously optimistic given her imaging results. Patient education reinforced regarding when to call with symptoms. Psychosocial support offered; she declined formal social work referral at this time but has strong family support.

Overall disease status: Cancer present (residual disease in right upper lobe and two intracranial lesions), actively responding to clinical trial therapy. Patient is in confirmed partial response at 8 weeks on savolitinib plus osi. Prognosis continues to be guarded given Stage IV disease with mixed acquired resistance, but response to the trial combination is favorable and patient is doing well clinically.

Encounter time: Approximately 45 minutes (history, exam, imaging review, plan discussion, patient education, protocol documentation).

Electronically signed by: Thoracic Oncology Attending
Trial Principal Investigator / Treating Physician
